commands.review_summary:
This PR moves Discord native built-in ownerAllowFrom checks before command menus/dispatch, changes command-arg copy from selected to applying, and adds focused auth/autocomplete regression tests.

PR surface: Source +26, Tests +126. Total +152 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main clears the Discord arg menu with a success-like Selected message before dispatch, while shared final auth can reject ownerAllowFrom non-owners and leave directives unapplied; no live Discord reproduction was run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blockers are contributor/maintainer real Discord proof and explicit acceptance of stricter auth behavior, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: fix(discord): gate native built-ins before success UI
target.body:
Closes #86654

## Summary
- gate native Discord built-in commands through the stricter owner allowlist auth path before menu/success UI is shown
- avoid optimistic command-arg success messaging by changing `Selected ...` to `Applying ...`
- add a regression test for the native slash `ownerAllowFrom` rejection path

## Root cause
The native Discord built-in command flow could acknowledge a selection before the downstream built-in auth path had actually accepted the directive. When that later auth rejected the directive, the interaction could fall through to the generic `✅ Done.` fallback, while the target session state stayed unchanged.

## Test evidence
- `pnpm vitest run extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command.commands-allowfrom.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command.options.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command.plugin-dispatch.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command.think-autocomplete.test.ts`
- result: 31/31 tests passing

## Rollback
Revert this PR to restore the previous native built-in command flow and picker copy.
